seoTitle: James Well vs Jamestown — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between James Well and Jamestown. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: James Well vs Jamestown: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in James Well and Jamestown.
heading: James Well vs Jamestown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between James Well, South Australia and Jamestown, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: James Well has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Jamestown (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is James Well or Jamestown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: James Well has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Jamestown scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: James Well scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Jamestown scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in James Well or Jamestown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. James Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
